clinical_history: Which one of the following statements about this ECG from an elderly man with metastatic prostate cancer and dehydration is not correct?
diagnosis: d) Ventricular repolarization prolongation is not present. The QT interval (about 320 msec) is normal for the rate and prominent U waves are not seen. The borderline low limb lead QRS voltage is a non-specific finding here.  Statements a, b, c and e are all correct.  The serum potassium turned out to be normal despite the slight T wave peaking.
